Privacy

Privacy Policy

How Open Play Map handles account, contribution, and site data.

Last updated: June 15, 2026

Who we are

Open Play Map is a community-powered pickleball directory operated by Scoop Pickleball. The service helps players find and share information about open play locations, schedules, reviews, and photos.

Questions about this policy can be sent to scooppickleball@gmail.com.

Information we collect

  • Account information: email address, username, password credentials handled through Supabase Auth, optional skill level, optional bio, and account role.
  • Community contributions: submitted locations, court details, open play schedules, reviews, photos, reports, suggested edits, moderation status, and reward credit activity.
  • Technical information: browser and device information, IP address, logs, security events, page views, approximate usage data, cookies, and local storage needed for the service to work.
  • Messages: information you send when contacting us about support, privacy, copyright, reports, or moderation decisions.

How we use information

  • Provide the map, account features, photo uploads, reviews, leaderboards, reports, and moderation tools.
  • Review submissions before they appear publicly and keep location information useful for players.
  • Protect accounts, prevent spam, investigate abuse, and enforce the Terms of Use and Community Guidelines.
  • Communicate about account, support, privacy, copyright, safety, and service issues.
  • Improve performance, reliability, security, and product decisions for the site.

Public content

Open Play Map is built around community contributions. Approved locations, reviews, photos, and some profile information such as username and skill level may be visible to other users and visitors. Do not post private information about yourself or anyone else in a review, photo, location, report, or suggested edit.

How we share information

We do not sell personal information. We may share information with service providers that help operate the site, including hosting, database, authentication, storage, security, analytics, and email tools. We may also share information if required by law, to protect rights and safety, to investigate misuse, or as part of a business transfer involving the service.

Cookies and local storage

The site may use cookies, browser storage, and similar technologies to keep you signed in, remember preferences, prevent abuse, improve security, and understand site usage. You can control cookies through your browser settings, but some features may stop working if cookies or storage are disabled.

Data retention

We keep account information for as long as your account is active or as needed to provide the service. Public contributions may remain available after account changes if they are useful to the map, unless removal is required by law, privacy, copyright, safety, or moderation reasons. We may keep certain records for security, legal, fraud prevention, and backup purposes.

Your choices

You can update parts of your profile from the account page. You can also contact us to request access, correction, deletion, or review of personal information associated with your account. We may need to verify your identity before acting on a request.

Children

Open Play Map is not directed to children under 13, and users under 13 may not create accounts or submit personal information. If you believe a child under 13 has provided personal information, contact us so we can review and remove it where appropriate.

Security

We use reasonable administrative, technical, and organizational safeguards designed to protect information, including HTTPS, managed authentication, access controls, storage rules, and moderation tools. No online service can guarantee perfect security, so please use a strong unique password and contact us if you suspect unauthorized account activity.

Changes

We may update this policy as the service changes. The updated version will be posted on this page with a new last updated date. Continued use of the service after changes means the updated policy applies.
